commands.review_summary:
The PR updates Discord voice docs, Discord config UI hints, and generated bundled channel metadata examples from `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` to `openai/gpt-5.5`.

PR surface: Source 0, Docs 0, Generated 0. Total 0 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current Discord docs and config hints still use the legacy model example while current OpenAI/provider docs define `openai/gpt-5.5` as the canonical new-config route.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because the external PR is dirty against current main and contributor-side real behavior proof is still required.

target.title: docs(discord): use canonical Codex model ref
target.body:
## Summary

This PR updates the Discord voice docs and bundled config help so new Discord voice examples point at the canonical Codex-backed OpenAI model ref: `openai/gpt-5.5`.

The problem is stale guidance: current OpenClaw docs say ChatGPT/Codex subscription auth should sign in through `openai-codex` auth while keeping the model ref as `openai/gpt-5.5`, but Discord voice docs and the generated config hint still showed `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` as the example brain model.

Intended outcome: users configuring Discord voice with OpenAI Realtime plus the OpenClaw Codex brain see the same canonical model route as the rest of the OpenAI/Codex docs.

Out of scope: no runtime/auth routing changes. The compaction auth routing bug appears already addressed on `main` by #86408; this PR only fixes stale Discord-facing docs/config metadata.

Success: `docs/channels/discord.md`, `extensions/discord/src/config-ui-hints.ts`, and generated bundled channel metadata all recommend `openai/gpt-5.5` for the Discord voice agent brain while leaving explicit legacy-route documentation elsewhere intact.

Reviewer focus: whether the Discord voice examples and config UI hint are the right places to canonicalize, and whether any remaining `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` references are intentional compatibility coverage.

AI-assisted: yes.

## Linked context

- Related: #86408 fixed the runtime compaction routing path on `main`.
- No issue number for this docs/config-hint cleanup.

## Real behavior proof required

- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord voice docs and the Discord `voice.model` config hint recommended legacy `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` for new Codex-backed OpenAI voice examples.
- Behavior addressed: new Discord voice guidance now uses `openai/gpt-5.5`, matching the current canonical OpenAI/Codex route described in `docs/providers/openai.md` and `docs/concepts/model-providers.md`.
- Real environment tested: local macOS source checkout at `/Users/mac/.openclaw/tmp/openclaw-pr-compaction-auth`, branch `codex/discord-codex-model-docs`; dependencies installed in the temporary clone with Corepack/pnpm cache redirected to `/private/tmp`. No production gateway restart was performed.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `env HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-home COREPACK_HOME=/private/tmp/corepack XDG_CACHE_HOME=/private/tmp/.cache PNPM_HOME=/private/tmp/pnpm pnpm config:channels:gen`
  - `env HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-home COREPACK_HOME=/private/tmp/corepack XDG_CACHE_HOME=/private/tmp/.cache PNPM_HOME=/private/tmp/pnpm pnpm config:channels:check`
  - `env HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-home COREPACK_HOME=/private/tmp/corepack XDG_CACHE_HOME=/private/tmp/.cache PNPM_HOME=/private/tmp/pnpm node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts src/config/validation.channel-metadata.test.ts`
  - `git diff --check`
  - `env HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-home COREPACK_HOME=/private/tmp/corepack XDG_CACHE_HOME=/private/tmp/.cache PNPM_HOME=/private/tmp/pnpm node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/config/bundled-channel-config-metadata.generated.ts`
  - `env HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-home COREPACK_HOME=/private/tmp/corepack XDG_CACHE_HOME=/private/tmp/.cache PNPM_HOME=/private/tmp/pnpm node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/discord/src/config-ui-hints.ts`
  - `HOME=/Users/mac codex review --base origin/main`
  - Partial: `env HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-home COREPACK_HOME=/private/tmp/corepack XDG_CACHE_HOME=/private/tmp/.cache PNPM_HOME=/private/tmp/pnpm pnpm check:changed`
- Evidence after fix:
  - `pnpm config:channels:check` exited 0.
  - Targeted Vitest exited 0: 2 files passed, 48 tests passed.
  - `git diff --check` exited 0.
  - Targeted oxlint commands for the changed generated metadata and Discord config hint exited 0.
  - `codex review --base origin/main` reported: `No actionable regressions were found. The Discord voice docs and config hint now match the canonical OpenAI model route, and the generated channel metadata check passes.`
  - `rg -n "openai-codex/gpt-5\.5|openai/gpt-5\.5" docs/channels/discord.md extensions/discord/src/config-ui-hints.ts src/config/bundled-channel-config-metadata.generated.ts` shows the changed Discord docs and hint surfaces now use `openai/gpt-5.5`; the generated Discord config hint has 0 `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` matches and 1 `openai/gpt-5.5` match.
- Observed result after fix: the public Discord voice examples and generated `voice.model` help text now point new users at `openai/gpt-5.5` for the Codex brain.
- What was not tested: no live Discord voice join, no live OpenAI Realtime session, and no production gateway restart. Full `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` was not run for this docs/config-hint change.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: `pnpm check:changed` completed through conflict-marker, changelog attribution, dependency/package guards, duplicate coverage, and core/extension typecheck lanes, then the core lint lane hit a `tsgolint` Go runtime SIGSEGV and was stopped after 177.46s. The narrower oxlint commands for the changed TS/generated files passed.
- Before evidence optional: before this patch, `docs/channels/discord.md` had six Discord voice examples/text references to `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, and `extensions/discord/src/config-ui-hints.ts` used `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` in the `voice.model` example.

## Tests and validation

- Passed: `pnpm config:channels:check`
- Passed: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts src/config/validation.channel-metadata.test.ts`
- Passed: `git diff --check`
- Passed: targeted `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` for `src/config/bundled-channel-config-metadata.generated.ts`
- Passed: targeted `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` for `extensions/discord/src/config-ui-hints.ts`
- Passed: `codex review --base origin/main` with no actionable regressions
- Partial: `pnpm check:changed`; failed/stopped in core lint after a `tsgolint` SIGSEGV, with earlier lanes green as described above

What failed before: stale docs/config hint text pointed at the legacy model route.

Regression coverage: targeted generated-metadata check plus Discord config schema and channel metadata tests cover the changed config metadata path.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior changed: docs/config help text only.
- Config/env/migration changed: no runtime config semantics, schema, migration, or env behavior changed; only generated config metadata text changed.
- Security/auth/secrets/network/tool exec changed: no.
- Highest risk: accidentally implying legacy `openai-codex/*` routes are unsupported. Mitigation: this PR only updates new-user Discord examples and leaves explicit legacy compatibility docs/tests in place.
- Mitigation: generated metadata was regenerated and checked; targeted tests/lints passed; Codex review reported no actionable regressions.

## Current review state

Draft PR. Maintainer edits enabled. AI-assisted and locally reviewed with `codex review --base origin/main`.

commands.author: rifachnia

target.title: fix: avoid gateway daemon repair on protocol mismatch
target.body:
## Summary

* Detect protocol mismatch explicitly during doctor gateway health checks
* Avoid treating incompatible running gateways as dead/stale gateways
* Skip daemon repair flow for protocol mismatch states
* Add regression coverage for protocol mismatch handling and repair skipping

Related #87156

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed:

* Protocol mismatch previously propagated as `healthOk=false`, causing `openclaw doctor` to treat a running gateway as dead/stale and enter daemon repair flow.

Real environment tested:

* Native Windows 11
* Git/source checkout
* Existing gateway service on port 18789
* Startup-folder fallback runtime active

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
openclaw gateway status
npm run openclaw -- doctor --deep
```

Evidence after fix:

* `gateway status` still reported:

  * `Connectivity probe: failed`
  * `protocol mismatch`
* but `doctor --deep`:

  * completed successfully
  * no longer printed `Gateway not running`
  * did not trigger daemon repair/reinstall flow

Observed result:

* Protocol mismatch is now treated as an incompatibility state instead of a dead gateway state.

Not tested:

* Scheduled Task migration flows
* Automatic gateway restart after update
* Non-Windows environments

## Tests

```bash
npm test -- src/commands/doctor-gateway-health.test.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-daemon-flow.test.ts

npm run typecheck

npm run lint
```

Regression coverage added:

* protocol mismatch health classification
* skipping daemon repair flow during protocol mismatch

Validation result:

* targeted protocol mismatch tests pass
* oxlint clean
* no new typecheck errors in touched files
* one unrelated existing Windows test failure remains (`/tmp/` hardcoded handoff env expectation)
